text: An initial survey of the pleural cavity was performed.
On retracting the lung just above the oblique fissure, one will notice multiple hydrated cysts coming into view.
The interlobar adhesions were gradually tackled with ultrasonic shears.
Retraction is of utmost importance here, as in any surgery.
Multiple gauze pieces soaked with 1% cetramide solution were introduced to cordon off the area of dissection.
The cyst was subsequently aspirated, the pericyst was incised, and the germinal membrane was delivered specifically onto the gauze piece to prevent any dissemination.
The geminal membrane was then placed meticulously within a sterile endobag.
Individual membranes were plucked, delivered, and placed in the sterile endobag repeatedly.
This meticulous delivery helps to restrict widespread dissemination of hydrated disease and prevent possibly lethal anaphylactic reactions.
Addictions between the hydrated cyst seen superiorly here and the pericardium are evident, which were gradually tackled with ultrasonic shears.
The cyst was punctured accurately, and the germinal membrane delivered out in a secure manner, which is of utmost importance.
A few other additions were separated.
Dissection here is particularly precarious because of the movement caused by the beating heart.
But pericardial injury was avoided in our particular case.
I believe this is the last hydrated cyst, the germinal membrane, which is again being delivered onto the gauze piece.
Delivery of this membrane was followed by flushing the cavity with hypotonic saline.
And finally, an intercostal chest strain was placed after delivery of the germinal membranes within the endobag.
An initial survey of the abdomen was performed.
Access was gained to the spleen by the lesser sac going through the gastrocolic ligament.
An inspection within the lesser sac demonstrated multiple adhesions between the splenic cyst and the lateral abdominal wall as well as the splenic flexure of the colon seen here.
This only further encouraged us for completing a spleen-preserving surgery.
The cyst was finally aspirated, which confirmed its position, and the access was dilated.
The contents were suctioned out with the use of a suction device.
The germinal membranes of the smaller daughter cysts were delivered as earlier.
The soft gelatinous consistency of the cyst walls actually necessitates atraumatic instrumentation and extremely gentle handling.
The germinal membrane of the mother cyst is now being pulled into view.
You can see it is especially large.
And as soon as it was delivered, it was again maneuvered into the endobag.
An inspection of the cyst cavity shows the presence of multiple daughter cysts.
We made a decision at this point in time to burst the cysts with a suction device and aspirate them from within the cyst cavity itself, reducing the amount of spillage.
And finally, an examination of the cyst cavity is performed to ensure clearance.
This is the most important step of the procedure as any dorsal cysts or parts of germinal membranes being left behind may lead to a recurrence, which is extremely common in our parts of the country.
Finally, the cyst cavity is flushed with the 1% cetramide solution and followed by a normal saline lavage as well.
The cavity is obliterated by an omentoplasty
